followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I revised the verdict upward from weak_evidence to routine after checking the PDF-level content: the phase equations, mass/SNR cuts, prior range [-5,5], and Table I numerical values are present in the actual paper even though the supplied HTML text stripped them. My remaining objections are therefore focused on modeling assumptions, reproducibility, and interpretation rather than missing core content.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: The objection that Eqs. (2)-(3), the prior range, and Table I are absent is not a paper-level flaw once the PDF is inspected; it is mainly an HTML extraction/rendering artifact.

The claim that the Johannsen citation is necessarily erroneous is weak: arXiv:1501.02809 itself lists the journal reference Phys. Rev. D 88, 044002 (2013), so the date/arXiv mismatch is not enough to sustain a citation-error objection.
Calling the simultaneous two-parameter degeneracy 'well supported' by this manuscript is too strong; it is plausible and inherited from prior work, but no joint-posterior evidence is shown here.
The alleged Table I contradiction is better framed as unclear presentation: the rows remain for alpha13 while the weak epsilon3 entries are omitted, rather than the entire events being removed.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The dissipative-sector assumption is a real unresolved modeling caveat.
The work has low methodological novelty and is primarily a GWTC-4 data update.
The absence of a combined multi-event constraint is a meaningful limitation if the deformation parameters are intended to be universal.
The weak/prior-limited epsilon3 cases should be handled and displayed more transparently.
The electromagnetic-versus-GW comparison needs clearer systematic-error caveats.

main_claims.claim: The paper applies Johannsen deformation parameters alpha13 and epsilon3 as additive non-Kerr corrections to the GW phase through a ppE/post-Newtonian framework.
main_claims.notes: The PDF contains the advertised equations, but the derivation is deferred to prior work and at least one coefficient/symbol, kappa, is not defined in the local text. I cannot certify the transcription or PN bookkeeping.

main_claims.claim: The analysis uses 11 new GWTC-4 BBH events selected for inspiral sensitivity, with cuts including detector-frame total mass M < 80 solar masses and network SNR > 10.
main_claims.notes: The mass and SNR thresholds are visible in the PDF, and the event list is given. The spin-precession/chi_p selection is still vague, and the paper does not provide a table of event masses, SNRs, chi_p values, frequency ranges, PSD choices, or sampler settings sufficient for reproduction.

main_claims.claim: When alpha13 and epsilon3 are varied one at a time, the reported posteriors are consistent with zero.
main_claims.notes: The reported 99% intervals visible in the PDF include zero for the displayed one-at-a-time constraints. Four epsilon3 cases are omitted as weak/prior-range-exceeding, which should be presented more transparently but does not by itself contradict the no-deviation conclusion.
main_claims.paper_location: Section III, Fig. 1 and Table I

main_claims.claim: GWTC-4 yields tighter individual constraints than the previous GWTC-3 analysis.
main_claims.notes: The current Table I values plausibly support improvement for the best events, especially GW230627 for alpha13, but the manuscript does not give a direct numerical side-by-side comparison to GWTC-3 or a combined catalog-level bound.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Section III; Concluding Remarks; Fig. 2

main_claims.claim: Simultaneously varying both deformation parameters leaves them unconstrained due to strong degeneracy.
main_claims.notes: This is plausible and consistent with the authors' prior paper, but this manuscript does not show the joint posterior, correlation coefficients, or quantitative diagnostics.
main_claims.paper_location: Section III, paragraph beginning 'Allowing both parameters to vary simultaneously...'

main_claims.claim: GW constraints on alpha13 have reached the constraining power of electromagnetic constraints.
main_claims.notes: Fig. 2 visually supports a broad comparison, but the paper does not tabulate the plotted 3-sigma intervals or discuss cross-method systematics, source-population differences, or whether the same effective parameter is being compared in a like-for-like way.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The omitted dissipative-sector deviations are negligible relative to the retained conservative Johannsen phase corrections for the selected GWTC-4 events.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The gravitational-wave phase is highly sensitive to radiation reaction; if flux modifications are comparable, the reported alpha13 and epsilon3 bounds are not clean constraints on the stated non-Kerr deformation model.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A single deformation parameter per event is a valid effective description of a binary with two compact objects.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this mapping, the inference result is hard to interpret as a Kerr-hypothesis constraint on astrophysical black holes rather than on an unspecified phenomenological phase coefficient.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The uniform prior range [-5,5] is physically appropriate and does not dominate the displayed posteriors except in the explicitly omitted weak epsilon3 cases.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Prior-range truncation can make a weak event look constrained or can affect comparisons across events and with GWTC-3.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The chosen 11-event selection and omitted weak epsilon3 results do not bias the improvement claim relative to GWTC-3.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The headline 'improved constraints' depends on comparable selection rules, SNR distributions, and treatment of low-information events.

missing_assumptions.assumption: IMRPhenomXPHM waveform systematics, detector calibration choices, and cleaned-channel data conditioning are small compared with the quoted deformation-parameter uncertainties.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Systematic phase errors can be degenerate with small beyond-Kerr phase corrections.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The alpha13 constraints inferred from GW and electromagnetic observations are comparable as constraints on the same physical parameter with compatible systematic-error treatment.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Otherwise the claim that GW tests have reached X-ray constraining power is mainly a visual scale comparison, not a robust methodological conclusion.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do Eqs. (2)-(3) exactly match the intended Carson-Yagi/Das-Shashank-Bambi phase corrections, including the definition of kappa, the definition of u, eta powers, signs, and PN order?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The equations are visible in the PDF but not derivable from the short manuscript text; checking them requires specialist comparison to the prior derivation.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the single-parameter treatment of alpha13 and epsilon3 in a BBH waveform theoretically well-defined for two potentially non-Kerr compact objects?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This depends on the underlying effective-one-body/ppE mapping and on assumptions inherited from the previous paper, which are not spelled out here.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Would including dissipative-sector deviations or performing injection-recovery studies materially change the quoted constraints?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Answering this requires calculations or simulations beyond the presented manuscript.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Can the event list and Table I be reproduced from public GWTC-4 data using the stated cuts, channels, priors, and Bilby/Dynesty setup?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper lacks enough run-configuration detail to reproduce the inference without additional author-provided settings or code.

novelty_assessment: The novelty is modest. The authors explicitly reuse the theoretical and waveform framework from their GWTC-3 work and apply it to 11 new GWTC-4 events. That is a legitimate catalog-update contribution, and the best individual GWTC-4 bounds appear useful, but the paper does not introduce a new derivation, waveform model, degeneracy-breaking strategy, or combined-event statistical treatment.
score_rationale: The paper is more solid than my cycle-1 HTML-only read suggested, because the PDF contains the central formulas and numerical results. Still, it remains a concise and incremental catalog update with unresolved modeling assumptions and limited reproducibility detail. The one-at-a-time no-deviation result is credible, but the broader Kerr-test interpretation and GW-versus-EM comparison require more caveats and human spot-checking.
summary: This is a short, incremental GWTC-4 update of the authors' earlier Johannsen-metric/ppE-inspired Kerr-deviation analysis. After checking the PDF-level content, several of my cycle-1 concerns about missing equations, priors, cuts, and table values are downgraded from manuscript flaws to HTML-rendering artifacts. The paper does provide the phase terms, uses M < 80 solar masses, network SNR > 10, uniform deformation-parameter priors in [-5,5], and reports one-at-a-time 99% intervals. The remaining concerns are more substantive: the conservative-only phase model is not justified locally, the single effective deformation parameter in a two-black-hole system is not clearly defined, waveform/frequency-range systematics are underspecified, and the strongest claims about improvement and electromagnetic comparison are only partially quantified.

technical_objections.evidence: After Eqs. (2)-(3), the paper defines eta, u, and f, but not kappa, even though kappa multiplies all displayed non-Kerr phase corrections. This makes the equations less self-contained and leaves the scale of the deformation terms opaque without consulting prior work.
technical_objections.objection: The phase-correction equations use kappa without defining it locally.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section II.A, Eqs. (2)-(3) and text immediately following

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states that dissipative effects remain unchanged from GR at leading order, but does not derive this for Johannsen-deformed binaries, cite a validation specific to these events, or estimate the bias from omitted flux/radiation-reaction corrections.
technical_objections.objection: The assumption that non-Kerr effects enter only through conservative-sector phase corrections is not justified enough for the claimed interpretation as a Kerr-hypothesis test.

technical_objections.evidence: The inference varies a single deformation parameter per event while discussing binary black holes. The paper does not state whether both bodies share a universal deformation, whether one effective parameter is being fit, or how asymmetric component deformations would enter the waveform.
technical_objections.objection: The mapping from two possible compact-object deformations in a BBH to one event-level alpha13 or epsilon3 parameter is not specified.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper says the corrections are incorporated into a frequency-domain IMRPhenomXPHM model, but does not state the frequency interval over which the non-Kerr terms are applied, how merger-ringdown frequencies are treated, or whether injection-recovery/waveform-systematic checks were done.
technical_objections.objection: The implementation of PN inspiral corrections inside the IMRPhenomXPHM waveform is underspecified.

technical_objections.evidence: The PDF gives M < 80 solar masses, SNR > 10, and deformation priors [-5,5], but the chi_p/precession criterion is not numerically stated, and no per-event selection table, PSD/frequency choices, data segment lengths, calibration assumptions, or Dynesty convergence settings are reported.
technical_objections.objection: Event selection and inference settings are still not fully reproducible despite the recovered mass, SNR, and prior values.

technical_objections.evidence: Only individual-event one-at-a-time posteriors are shown. If alpha13 and epsilon3 are assumed to be universal deformation parameters, a combined posterior would be the natural dataset-level result; if they are not universal, the interpretation should be stated more carefully.
technical_objections.objection: The paper does not present a combined multi-event constraint, even though it emphasizes the improved GWTC-4 dataset and future universal-parameter combinations.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section III and final paragraph of Section IV

technical_objections.evidence: Four events are said to have epsilon3 99% intervals exceeding the analysis range and are omitted from Fig. 1 and Table I. Table I still lists those events for alpha13 while leaving the corresponding epsilon3 results absent, so readers cannot see how prior-dominated those posteriors were.
technical_objections.objection: The treatment of weak epsilon3 constraints is not transparent enough.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section III, Table I and paragraph discussing four omitted events

technical_objections.evidence: The text compares 3-sigma constraints from GW and electromagnetic methods, while Table I reports 99% intervals for the GW event results. The paper does not tabulate the Fig. 2 values or discuss method-dependent systematics in X-ray reflection, continuum fitting, EHT imaging, and GW waveform modeling.
technical_objections.objection: The electromagnetic-versus-GW comparison does not control for different systematics or interval definitions.
